title: HIV-1 Genotyping and Drug Resistance by Next Generation Sequencing
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"fplrt","text":"The Florida Department of Health's Bureau of Public Health Laboratories in Jacksonville is developing a protocol for the Next Generation Sequencing (NGS) of HIV, primarily for the purpose of drug-resistant mutation identification. This HIV-1 protocol uses amplicon based sequencing. The amplified pol gene regions are used in both genotyping and drug resistance determination. The NGS data generated can also be used in surveillance and outbreak monitoring, giving epidemiologist more information about circulating viral genomes.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"italic","offset":297,"length":3}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fk2e","text":"The imminent sunsetting of ViroSeq (Abbott Molecular) has required many labs to look for new methods to continue identifying HIV-1 drug resistance strains for both clinical management and epidemiological study. NGS was chosen as it is more cost effective than investing in a single pathogen platform. NGS allows for one sample to produce results and data that can aid not just a patient but an entire population. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Extract RNA using the Qiagen Viral RNA Mini Kit (DSP or RUO)

Master Mix with Invitrogen SuperScript III One-Step RT-PCR System with Platinum Taq High Fidelity DNA Polymerase (cat 12574-035) 25 µL, 2x Reaction Mix 1.0 µL, HIV2252-F 1.0 µL, HIV5073-R 1.0 µL, SuperScript III Platinum Taq 2.5 µL, DMSO 19.5 µL, viral RNA template

Master Mix with Invitrogen SuperScript III One-Step RT-PCR System with Platinum Taq High Fidelity DNA Polymerase (cat 12574-035) 25 µL, 2x Reaction Mix 1.0 µL, FL_HIV_Ext Nest FW 1.0 µL, FL_HIV_Ext Nest RV 1.0 µL, SuperScript III Platinum Taq 2.5 µL, DMSO 19.5 µL, clean cDNA template
Quantitate Samples and dilute as needed to achieve 1.0 ng input concentration per sample
Amplicon quality can effect how many samples can be pooled onto one run. Use caution in deciding how many samples to pool.

We currently use SmartGene HIV-1 pipeline ( https://www.smartgene.com/ ) for analysis Pipeline Name: HIV-1 PR+RT+IN Version 2.2.0 HIV1 V1.6 Noise Filter [%] 0.5 Interpretation cut off [%] 5.0 Minimum read depth and additional criteria should be determined by your institution

Dudley DM, Bailey AL, Mehta SH, Hughes AL, Kirk GD, Westergaard RP, O'Connor DH. Cross-clade simultaneous HIV drug resistance genotyping for reverse transcriptase, protease, and integrase inhibitor mutations by Illumina MiSeq. Retrovirology. 2014 Dec 23;11:122. doi: 10.1186/s12977-014-0122-8. PMID: 25533166; PMCID: PMC4302432.
Add 5.6 µL, carrier RNA/ sample
Follow Kit Instructions
Reverse-Transcription/Amplification PCR thermocycler settings 105 °C, Lid setting 50 °C 00:30:00 15 cycles 94 °C 00:00:15 55 °C 00:00:30 68 °C 00:03:00 Final extension 78 °C 00:05:00 4 °C, Hold
Using a ratio of 0.5 follow the AMPure XP bead protocol for PCR purification
Second Amplification PCR thermocycler settings 105 °C, Lid setting 40 cycles 94 °C 00:00:15 55 °C 00:00:30 68 °C 00:03:00 Final extension 78 °C 00:05:00 4 °C, Hold
Using a ratio of 0.5 follow the AMPure XP bead protocol for PCR purification
Visualize samples using Tapestation or agarose gel
Fragments should be ~2.9 kB

If the 2.9 kb fragment is isolated then samples should sequence well. If there are additional fragments sequencing is still possible, but samples may require a higher input concentration to achieve desired coverage
Follow Illumina Protocol for Nextera XT DNA Library Sample Prep
Normalize samples prior to pooling by dilution
Samples should be pooled in an equal volume amount. If desired the pool can be cleaned using an additional AMPure XP bead clean.
Dilute the Pooled Library to apx. 0.07 ng/µL or a concentration found in the Illumina iSeq manual
Quantitate the Pooled Library
Add 5 µL 1 nanomolar (nM), PhiX to the pool
Load according to Illumina instructions
creator: Jax Sequencing
